text.title: Pricing Experiment Log Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Pricing Experiment Log application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every log row MUST carry a falsifiable hypothesis ('raising price by 20% will increase MRR by ≥10% over 30 days'); vague claims rejected.
rule.rationale: Falsifiable hypothesis is the difference between an experiment and folklore.

rule.statement: Every row MUST carry baseline MRR + conversion % at toggle time; rows without baseline are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without baseline, observed delta is uninterpretable.

rule.statement: Measurement window MUST be locked BEFORE toggle (e.g. 30 days); post-toggle extension of window rejected unless logged as new experiment.
Every closed row MUST carry one of {keep, revert, iterate} decisions with rationale; open-ended 'monitor' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Open-ended monitor = the toggle stays in place by default forever.

rule.statement: Every row MUST link to Stripe + dashboard URLs as evidence; rows without evidence are rejected.
